cards.front: wash, washing, laundry (act of cleaning with water)
mine (place where ore is taken out of the ground)
bee, insect of the clade Anthophila
man, human male, either adult or age-irrespective
human, any member of the species Homo sapiens
view, opinion (way of understanding or looking at something)
year (the time it takes the Earth to circle the Sun; its equivalent on other planets)
the function, duty of standing guard; a watch or vigil
an hour, a period of time
a hand of a human, other simian or other animal with fingers
hen, female chicken; female of a related species
a horn (sound device) or a bugle
a thing, matter (general term with for a concept other than animate beings)
pace, step; also as a measure of distance
a school, educational institution that provides education, whether combined with research or not
a set of intended actions, through which one expects to achieve a goal: plan, plot, scheme
a room, physical chamber in a building
something which is happening or which happened; event
an orphan, person or animal having survived both parents
dad (term of address for one’s father, especially used by young children)
a deer, a ruminant of the family Cervidae; sometimes used of members of the families Moschidae and Tragulidae in non-scientific use (see derived terms)
murder, a type of killing legally in a more severe degree than homicide
seeing, the ability to observe visually, vision, sight
parent, father or mother; by extension a substitute caregiver
number used as a means of identification
a fall (act or event of falling)
datum, a given fact, parameter, piece of information
research, experiment, study, trial, test, survey
a piece, part, share (of a whole)
a person who is kind or sweet; a sweetheart
operating theatre, operating room (room where surgical procedures are performed)
a film, thin layer or membrane; especially the physical medium film
captain (person in command of a ship)
a jail, a prison; especially an institution where convicts are incarcerated, but sometimes used more generally
a steering device, notably a steering wheel in a vehicle or handlebars on a bicycle
the dead but otherwise (mostly) intact body, particularly of a human but sometimes used of animals
genome (complete genetic information of an organism)
space, room (for a physical object to occupy)
meter (device that measures things or indicates a physical quantity)
a recreational drug, psychoactive substance, especially when illegal and addictive
a young: a young being, especially an immature animal
line of writing, verse; (pre-printed) straight line to write it on
sack or sack-like container, such as a bag, sachet, pouch
voice, sound made by the mouth using airflow
choice, whether already made or not
bond, connection, relationship, liaison, tie (attachment, as in a relation)
full general (rank higher than a colonel)
partner, love interest, romantic and/or sexual companion
flight (the act of flying, an instance thereof)
a purulent boil, skin injury containing pus, ulcer
destructive, catastrophic fire (such as a house fire)
club (an association of members joining together for some common purpose, especially sports or recreation)
a prick (insect bite), a stab, a thrust
a realm, kingdom ruled by a monarch
a ball or any object with such a shape
an act of aggression; assault, attack
shop, store (establishment that sells goods to the public)
sergeant (non-commissioned officer in several armed forces)
animal (any member of the kingdom Animalia)
aunt (sister or sister-in-law of a parent)
a pair of trousers, pair of pants
history (that which happened in the past, especially insofar as it is relevant for later times)
wine (an alcoholic beverage produced by a certain fermentation procedure; unless otherwise specified or indicated by context, made from grapes)
an excuse, apology, explanation to avoid or alleviate guilt or negative judgement
leg, limb of a person, horse (other animals would have poten) and certain objects (again many have poten)
a monster, terrifying and dangerous creature
turn (a chance to use (something) shared in sequence with others)
patient, someone who receives therapeutic treatment
basis (principle, foundation, that which is elementary)
domestic cat, pussy, Felis silvestris catus
governing (act of; ruling a state or similar political entity)
a chair, a piece of furniture used for sitting, normally portable and having four legs
projectile: bullet, shot (heavy iron ball used for the shot put)
role, function (as in actions or tasks that a person or a group is expected to perform)
experience (act of experiencing, something that is experienced)
age (amount of time a being or object has lived or existed)
a lunch, a meal eaten around noon
book or body of laws, code of laws, lawbook
stilt (either of two poles with footrests that allow someone to stand or walk above the ground)
beer (alcoholic drink brewed from grains or other starch material)
task, assignment, an action that one is required to do
action, activity, movement; especially (without article) a fast-paced activity
director, manager (in a company), general manager, CEO (or at a lower (e.g., branch) level)
a breakfast, first meal of the day, usually in the morning
paper (A material used for writing or printing on, made from cellulose pulp rolled into thin sheets.)
a crude boor, lout, sull; the diminutive often lacks the derogatory connotation
mayor, burgomaster (head of a city or municipality)
university, (university) college (especially in the United States)
magic, sorcery (occult woo, black or white magic, etc.); hence supernatural occurrences or phenonomena
type: a class, someone or something from a class. The diminutive is used when made into a caricature
unit of mass, often broadly similar to 500 grams
target (something or someone at which one aims), prey
someone higher in rank; a superior or boss
a pie, large cake, or cobbler
a press, mechanical device to exert pression
minister (a politician who heads a ministry)
milk (nutritious liquid produced by a lactating mammalian mother)
a memory, something that one remembers
truck, lorry (heavy motor vehicle designed to carry goods)
SMS, a service for sending text messages on a cellular telephone system
Internet (specific internet consisting of the global network of computers)
Christmas, the major Christian feast commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ
bird, any member of the class Aves
a pill, a usually ball- or oval-shaped, coated portion of a drug to be taken orally
protection (state or quality of being protected)
container, such as a box, a crate, a tray or a tub
a farewell, an occasion of saying goodbye; a departure
storm; a wind scale for very strong wind, stronger than a gale, less than a hurricane
a hat, worn on the head
request (act of requesting), friendly demand, petition
cent, a subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of the Dutch guilder
a garage (repair shop for motorised vehicles)
reaction (action in response to an event)
group of people or animals (such as a pride of lions or a herd of cows)
language (form of communication consisting of vocabulary and grammar)
the government, the authorities (the state and its administration; governmental administration in the widest sense)
a stable, a place to keep hoofed animals
a flow, current or flush, as of moving water or other liquid
a teacher, a person who gives lessons
sin (offence against moral and/or divine law)
the sparkling wine champagne from the French region Champagne
driver (person who drives a motorized vehicle, such as a car or a bus; usually to transport others or in a professional capacity, often both)
a betrothed, a fiancée or fiancé
pension (annuity paid regularly as benefit due to a retired employee)
a physical market place, usually a public square
the centre, middle of focal part (e.g. of activity)
a bear, any member of the family Ursidae
a T-shirt or other shirt, typically including undershirts
compassion, sympathy for the suffering of another
wolf (Canis lupus; the largest wild member of the canine subfamily)
ellipsis of mobiele telefoon; a mobile phone, cellphone, mobile
a student at an institute for academic tertiary education
crew (group of people manning and operating a vehicle, piece of equipment or more rarely a facility)
training (imparting or acquisition of skills, esp. in exercise and sports, also in chiefly extracurricular education)
criminal (person guilty of breaking the law)
means, system or instrument for fulfilling an end
speech (act, manner or faculty of speaking)
desert (environmental area with very little precipitation)
a sod, turf of soil, grass, reed or other vegetation
a season, major part of the year
a pair of glasses or goggles
humanity, mankind (totality of humans, all members of the genus Homo)
route, course, way (particular pathway or direction one travels)
threat, menace (something that poses a risk)
homework (educational assignment to be done in one's own time)
monkey, ape, simian; any member of the infraorder Simiiformes
check, cheque (a note promising to pay money to a named person or entity)
winner, one who has won or often wins
doctor (person who has attained a doctorate)
snake, squamate of the suborder Serpentes
a mate, comrade, colleague, fellow, companion, (not very close) friend, ally etc.
brilliant (finely cut gemstone, especially a diamond)
a stupid person, fool (male or of unspecified gender)
spy, person who secretly gathers information
the subtitles to a video in a particular language (see usage note); sub
justice (the branch of government that holds the power of jurisdiction)
plant, any member of the kingdom Plantae
a long feather of a bird
interview (conversation intended for recording statements for publication)
female cousin; daughter of someone's uncle or aunt
fodder, grub, animal food, feed, (for humans) inferior food
invitation; the act of inviting; solicitation, the requesting of a person's company
chain (a series of interconnected rings or links)
a clinic, type of hospital or other medical facility for outpatient treatment
English or American mile, a unit of length equivalent to about 1.6 km
